slice: The just-shipped card-layout fix was correctly deployed, but the user's browser kept showing the old, broken layout after refreshing -- a real, separate gap this codebase had already learned to guard against everywhere else: the root Studio index.html has carried ?v=NNNN cache-busting query params on its own stylesheet links since an earlier, dedicated fix, but this file had never adopted the same convention on ANY of its own tags, CSS or JS, across its entire history. Confirmed via grep that every occurrence of .js"/.css" in the file is genuinely inside a <link>/<script src> tag before doing a global find-and-replace. Fixed by appending ?v=0095 (this sprint's own build number) to the stylesheet link and all 24 <script src> tags in index.html. Going forward, bump this one number on every future ship that touches CSS/JS, mirroring the root index.html's own established ritual. A real Playwright-harness gotcha was found and fixed while re-verifying, not an app bug: the existing test suites' page.route('**/themeRepositoryClient.js', ...) mocks stopped matching once the real URL gained a query string, since Playwright's glob pattern must match the full URL including its query -- widened to a trailing wildcard in both affected scripts, re-verified passing (14/14 and 8/8). A separate two-goldenBuild.js-failure scare during this same verification pass was traced to a leftover shell working directory from an earlier command, not a regression -- re-run from the repo root, both pass (30/30 each) unchanged. Verified via a fresh Playwright screenshot loading the real, versioned URLs end-to-end: zero console errors, the fixed two-row card layout renders correctly. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to index.html.
